title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 17% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, buoyed by dominant March qualifiers including a 4-0 rout of Ukraine to top their UEFA group, showcasing Mbappé-led attacking depth. Spain follows closely at 16% after thrashing Georgia 4-0 and securing Group E, leveraging Euro pedigree and Yamal's emergence, while England's 11% reflects steady wins like 2-0 over Serbia. The tight top cluster stems from the expanded 48-team format's group stage dynamics—top-two plus eight best third-placed advances—favorable draws for elites (France vs. Senegal, Norway; manageable paths for others), host boosts for USA, Mexico, Canada, and qualifier surprises like Bosnia's penalty shootout over Italy, fostering uncertainty despite no major injuries disrupting form ahead of June 11 kickoff.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the US, Israel, and Iran, announced April 8 and since extended indefinitely by President Trump pending Tehran's unified proposal, has halted major airstrikes and missile exchanges but faces strain from Strait of Hormuz incidents. On April 22, Iran's Revolutionary Guard seized two vessels and attacked others, prompting Trump on April 23 to order US forces to "shoot and kill" small Iranian boats deploying mines while intensifying mine-clearing. Diplomatic efforts, including paused US talks in Pakistan mediated by Islamabad and a three-week Israel-Lebanon truce extension, offer de-escalation paths amid blockade enforcement and proxy tensions, leaving traders to weigh resumption risks against negotiation breakthroughs.

title: Nuggets vs. Timberwolves
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 23 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Nuggets win, the market will resolve to "Nuggets".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves lead the Western Conference first-round series 2-1 after a commanding 113-96 Game 3 win at Target Center on April 23, where Minnesota's defense stifled Denver to 39% shooting and forced 18 turnovers, fueling trader focus on the Wolves' home-court momentum heading into Game 4. Nuggets forward Aaron Gordon remains sidelined with left calf tightness—missing Game 3 and impacting wing defense against Anthony Edwards—while Peyton Watson (hamstring) is also out, thinning Denver's rotation amid Nikola Jokić's heavy minutes. Edwards played through right knee maintenance in Game 3, posting key scoring; Minnesota's recent form includes a narrow Game 2 road steal (119-114), offsetting Denver's Game 1 dominance (116-105). Back-to-back road tests challenge the Nuggets' rebounding edge and Jamal Murray's shoulder status.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom holds a commanding 26% implied probability as the trader consensus frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by recent California and New Hampshire polls showing him leading the field among primary voters, bolstered by his executive experience, national media profile from past Trump clashes, and term limit freeing him post-2026 midterms. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8.3% reflects a progressive surge demanding fresh leadership after Kamala Harris's 2024 defeat, while Harris at 7.6% gains from her recent signals of interest in another run and establishment ties. Jon Ossoff's 5.4% stems from Georgia incumbency advantages. Differentiation lies in Newsom's fundraising edge and swing-state viability versus AOC's youth and base mobilization or Harris's name recognition; consolidation could follow strong 2026 midterm showings by governors like Shapiro, Whitmer, or Beshear, key endorsements, or DNC shifts ahead of Iowa caucuses.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 64-18 regular season record and No. 1 overall seed with home-court advantage throughout the playoffs drive their commanding 51.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite for the 2026 NBA Championship. The defending champs opened the first round with a 2-0 lead over the Phoenix Suns, powered by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's dominance, though Jalen Williams' hamstring injury in Game 2 introduces minor depth concerns. San Antonio Spurs (11.4%) trail closely after a strong 62-20 campaign but sit 1-1 versus Portland following Victor Wembanyama's concussion setback. Boston Celtics (11.8%) hold solid East No. 2 positioning yet dropped Game 2 to Philadelphia, highlighting vulnerabilities amid Tatum's ramp-up from injury, while Denver and Cleveland lurk with favorable paths.

title: US x Iran ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran announced on April 7, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between the United States and Iran, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 7 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect. 

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed two-week period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced on April 21, 2026, an indefinite extension of the US-Iran ceasefire—originally a two-week truce mediated by Pakistan starting April 8—pending submission of a unified Iranian proposal amid stalled negotiations in the ongoing 2026 Iran war. The US maintains its naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz, while Tehran has not publicly confirmed agreement, fueling uncertainty despite de-escalation signals. Recent tensions include Iranian ship seizures and proxy clashes, with no new talks scheduled but diplomats urging progress to avert resumption of airstrikes or military escalation. Trader consensus reflects this fragile diplomatic posture, where Iran's response and potential breakthroughs could rapidly shift probabilities.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: A Pakistan-mediated ceasefire between the United States and Iran, agreed on April 8 following U.S. and Israeli strikes, has held amid stalled negotiations for a permanent peace deal encompassing nuclear curbs, sanctions relief, regional security guarantees, and Strait of Hormuz access protocols. Marathon talks in Islamabad on April 10-12 ended without agreement after Iran rejected U.S. demands for an ironclad no-nuclear-weapons commitment, prompting Vice President JD Vance to walk out. President Trump extended the truce indefinitely on April 21 at Pakistan's request, but a second round remains on hold amid mixed signals from Tehran and heightened rhetoric from both sides. Upcoming diplomatic efforts hinge on Iran's response to U.S. proposals, with economic pressure via blockades persisting.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' dominant 2026 car has propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of the drivers' standings after four Grands Prix, with Antonelli leading at 72 points to Russell's 63 following his Japan GP victory—his second win after China—while Russell took Australia. This 1-2 team dynamic and superior race pace (0.6s edge) explain their implied probabilities far ahead of rivals like Charles Leclerc (49 points, Ferrari). Traders favor Russell's championship experience and consistency despite trailing points, viewing the intra-team battle as the key decider amid Ferrari's closing gap and upcoming Miami GP challenges like track evolution and tire strategy.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. narrowly ahead at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, with Vice President J.D. Vance close at 39% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 20%, underscoring an open primary field shaped by President Trump's term limits and his likely endorsement power. HHS Secretary Kennedy's recent midterm travel push promoting the Make America Healthy Again agenda since early April has amplified his populist appeal on chronic disease and vaccine skepticism, drawing base enthusiasm beyond traditional polls where Vance leads (e.g., CPAC straw poll 53%, Echelon Insights 42%). Vance's fundraising blitz with top GOP donors reinforces his institutional edge as heir apparent. The tight race persists amid divergent polling and market signals; separation could emerge from 2026 midterm results, early Iowa caucus polling, or explicit Trump backing ahead of primaries starting in 2027.

title: Avalanche vs. Kings
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 23 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Avalanche win, the market will resolve to "Avalanche".
If the Kings win, the market will resolve to "Kings".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche hold a commanding 3-0 series lead over the Los Angeles Kings in their Western Conference first-round Stanley Cup Playoffs matchup following a narrow Game 3 road victory at Crypto.com Arena on April 23, underscoring their resilience in tight contests after Game 1's defensive stand and Nicolas Roy's overtime winner in Game 2. Avalanche goaltender Scott Wedgewood's strong play (24 saves in Game 1) and a fully healthy roster—including Nazem Kadri's return from a finger injury—bolster trader consensus on Colorado's dominance as the Pacific Division's top seed with superior depth. Kings face elimination in Game 4 on April 26 amid injuries to forwards Kevin Fiala, Andrei Kuzmenko, and Alex Turcotte, plus coaching staff ailments from a Game 2 bench skirmish, though home-ice advantage and desperation could spark a rally in this historically close rivalry.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 99.5% implied probability for no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, anchored by resilient March 2026 economic data and hawkish FOMC minutes. Nonfarm payrolls rose 178,000—beating expectations—while CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year, the highest since May 2024, fueled by war-related oil shocks in the Middle East. Released April 8, minutes affirmed the 3.50-3.75% target range, with some officials signaling openness to hikes amid elevated inflation risks, echoing Chair Powell's comments on geopolitical uncertainty. With minimal data ahead of the decision, this strong positioning holds firm, though a surprise inflation flare-up could challenge the pause.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 24?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 17 - April 24, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 17 12:00 PM ET to April 24, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 71.5% implied probability to Elon Musk posting 200-219 times on X from April 17-24, 2026, fueled by his steady pace of 28 posts per day through seven elapsed days, totaling 195 as of early April 24 per XTracker data—projecting squarely into that bucket with room for typical daily swings of 14-46 tweets. This reflects Musk's unrelenting cultural dominance via viral commentary on Tesla milestones like Cybercab production start, AI advancements, and political hot takes, sustaining high engagement without recent lulls. The 25.3% on 180-199 accounts for potential end-of-period slowdowns, though his morning activity today signals continued momentum ahead of market close. Unpredictable late surges remain a wildcard in this skin-in-the-game sentiment.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen has solidified its frontrunner status in trader consensus at 36.4% implied probability following their February UMK national final victory, with the dance-pop track's virtuoso violin showcase and chaotic live energy drawing rave previews for televote dominance akin to past winners like Loreen. Bookmakers echo this lead, citing the song's catchy hooks and staging potential amid all 35 entries now released. Denmark (11.8%) and France (11.5%) trail closely on strong melodic entries and recent wagering surges, despite Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund facing minor backlash over a pro-Israel post; Greece, Australia, and Israel round out contenders with upbeat anthems. Momentum could shift with fan polls and Vienna previews ahead of May semis.

title: Next French Presidential Election
description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Following March 2026 municipal elections, where Rassemblement National gained ground in cities like Marseille and Nice—perceived as strengthened by 75% of voters—trader consensus prices Édouard Philippe and Jordan Bardella nearly even atop a fragmented field for the 2027 presidential race. Recent polls show Bardella leading first-round voting intentions at 34-38%, buoyed by widespread desire for change on security, immigration, and economy, while Philippe scores 18-26% but edges runoffs at 51.5-52% via center-right consolidation against far-right advance. Jean-Luc Mélenchon's left-wing base holds at 12-15%. With Macron term-limited, the race stays tight amid party struggles for unifying candidates; separations could arise from primaries, endorsements, or scandals before April 2027 first round.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance edges California Governor Gavin Newsom as the trader-favored frontrunners for the 2028 presidential election, reflecting an open race after President Trump's term limit bars a third bid. Vance's lead stems from his March CPAC straw poll dominance at 53%, surging donor outreach with major Republican contributors, and a new book fueling 2028 speculation, alongside polls like UMass Lowell showing him ahead in hypotheticals. Newsom counters with commanding California Democratic primary leads and a New Hampshire tie, positioning him as the party's early standard-bearer. Diverging views on Iran between Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio underscore GOP tensions, keeping odds tight below 20%. 2026 midterms in swing states and early primary endorsements could widen gaps.

title: Which company has the best AI model end of April?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Score" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ranked primarily by their arena score at this market’s check time, with alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's release of Claude Opus 4.7 on April 16 has cemented its dominance on key AI benchmarks, driving trader consensus to a 90.5% implied probability of holding the top large language model spot by April's end per Polymarket's skin-in-the-game wagers. The model leads the LMArena text and coding leaderboards with an Elo rating of 1504, excelling in long-horizon reasoning, agentic workflows, and SWE-Bench Pro scores 20% ahead of rivals like OpenAI's GPT-5.4, despite recent launches from Google (Gemini 3.1 Pro) and xAI (Grok 4.20). With only days until resolution based on blind human-voted arenas, a surprise late-April model drop from OpenAI or Google surpassing Claude on multiple metrics could realistically challenge this positioning, though historical release lags temper such expectations.

title: NFL Champion 2027
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2027 NFL league championship. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2027 NFL league championship per the rules of the NFL (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2027 NFL league championship game is cancelled, postponed after March 31, 2027 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from NFL (https://www.nfl.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Seahawks lead trader consensus for NFL Champion 2027 at 12.5% implied probability as defending Super Bowl LX winners, with Mike Macdonald's squad retaining core talent like Kenneth Walker III and benefiting from mid-round draft hits such as Boye Mafe amid a deep roster poised for repeat contention. NFC West rival Los Angeles Rams follow closely at 9.5% after bolstering their lineup through March free agency moves, while Buffalo Bills' 7.5% reflects Josh Allen's elite quarterback production and defensive continuity. The race remains tight across a wide-open field, fueled by parity post-free agency, impending 2026 NFL Draft trades and selections—highlighted by Seahawks' interest in moving back from No. 32 pick—and multiple contenders like Ravens, Chiefs, and Chargers showing offseason upside without dominant dynasty threats.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and a fragile ceasefire extended by President Trump on April 21, trader sentiment reflects skepticism over near-term US-Iran diplomatic progress following the first high-level direct talks in Islamabad on April 11-12. Led by Vice President JD Vance and mediated by Pakistan, those 21-hour negotiations ended without agreement despite reported foundational advancements, leaving gaps on sanctions relief, nuclear issues, and naval blockades. Iran has condemned US actions, signaling no firm date for a second round as rhetoric escalates; upcoming Vance-led discussions remain on hold, with de-escalation hinging on mutual concessions before any resolution deadline.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 97.2% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic returning to normal by April 30, reflecting persistent Iranian restrictions amid U.S.-Iran tensions that have slashed daily transits to single digits—versus pre-crisis norms of 20-30 vessels—from late March. Iran briefly reopened the strait on April 17 before reimposing controls on April 20, prompting sharp traffic drops confirmed by trackers like MarineTraffic and Windward, with just 2-11 vessels in recent 24-hour windows as of April 23. Heightened risks from IRGC threats and stalled ceasefire talks reinforce caution among shippers, stranding hundreds in the Gulf. Tail risks include a surprise diplomatic breakthrough or U.S. naval enforcement shift, though proximity to resolution favors the status quo.

title: U.S. forces seize another oil tanker by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if U.S. government forces seize an oil tanker or any other ship actively transporting oil between market creation and April 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

U.S. government forces refer to any active U.S. military (including U.S. Coast Guard), law enforcement, or intelligence personnel or contractors.

Seizes refers to U.S. forces taking custody of or asserting operational control of the vessel, including boarding and taking control of the vessel, detaining the vessel indefinitely, or forcefully rerouting the vessel to a U.S.-controlled port.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: U.S. forces seize another oil ship

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. forces seized the Guyana-flagged oil tanker Majestic X in the Indian Ocean on April 23, 2026, for transporting sanctioned Iranian crude as part of an aggressive maritime interdiction campaign targeting Tehran's shadow fleet, with the Pentagon releasing footage of the boarding operation. This marks the latest in a series of rapid seizures—including a third vessel within 24 hours earlier that week—amid escalating tensions after Iran's Revolutionary Guards seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and attacked others. The Trump administration's naval blockade enforces oil sanctions, disrupting illicit networks linked to Iran, Venezuela, and Russia. Traders eye potential further interdictions or Iranian retaliation, with no major diplomatic breakthroughs signaled ahead of possible Hormuz escalations.

title: Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's nominee Kevin Warsh holds commanding trader consensus at 94% for Fed Chair confirmation following his April 21 Senate Banking Committee hearing, where he pledged monetary policy independence, denied White House pressure for rate cuts, and outlined hawkish priorities like enhanced inflation data tracking. This advanced his nomination—formally sent March 4—past initial scrutiny from Democrats like Sen. Elizabeth Warren, bolstering expectations amid Republican Senate majority control. The path clears Jerome Powell ahead of his May 15 term end as chair, despite Sen. Thom Tillis's hold tied to a DOJ probe into Powell. Realistic challenges include prolonged holdout, filibuster failure on cloture, or late revelations on Warsh's finances or crypto ties shifting Senate votes.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil prices hover around $96–$98 per barrel amid acute supply disruptions from the ongoing US-Iran conflict and partial Strait of Hormuz closure, slashing global flows by up to 11 million bpd and flipping the market into deficit per IEA estimates. The latest EIA data for the week ending April 17 showed a surprise 1.925 million barrel inventory build versus expected draws, tempering gains, while US production holds steady near 13.5 million bpd. OPEC+ plans a modest 206,000 bpd output hike for April, but geopolitical risks dominate trader consensus. Watch next week's EIA report on April 29 and any escalation in Middle East tensions for volatility into month-end resolution.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori of Fuerza Popular secured first place in Peru's April 12-13 presidential first-round vote with around 17% amid a fragmented field of 35 candidates, positioning her for the June 7 runoff, while Roberto Sánchez Palomino of Juntos por el Perú climbed to second at 12% in the latest ONPE count of over 95% of regular actas. Logistical chaos, including ballot shortages and extended voting, slowed the tally, with 5.6% observed ballots pending Jurados Electorales Especiales resolution and final certification expected mid-May. Trader consensus favors Fujimori's victory due to her experienced campaign machinery from prior runs, strong regional base, and likely consolidation of right-leaning votes against Sánchez's late surge, amid ongoing fraud allegations from trailing candidates like Rafael López Aliaga.

title: 2026 Men's French Open Winner
description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's trader consensus at 55.5% implied probability for the 2026 Men's French Open title reflects his breakthrough Rolex Monte-Carlo Masters victory on April 12, defeating Carlos Alcaraz 7-6(5), 6-3 in the final for his maiden clay-court ATP Masters 1000 crown and reclaiming the PIF ATP No. 1 ranking. This dominant clay-swing start, following hard-court triumphs at Indian Wells and Miami, showcases improved baseline rallies and serve effectiveness on slower surfaces, boosting his Roland Garros prospects despite a modest prior best of quarterfinals. Alcaraz sits at 22.5% after the runner-up finish but faces headwinds from his Madrid Open withdrawal amid fitness concerns, while Novak Djokovic's shoulder injury clouds his 3% odds and Alexander Zverev's consistent clay pedigree holds him at 4.5%.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs and Portland Trail Blazers head into Game 3 of their first-round Western Conference playoff series tied 1-1, with Victor Wembanyama's concussion protocol status dominating trader focus after he exited Game 2 early via hard fall, enabling Portland's narrow 106-103 road upset despite Damian Lillard's season-long Achilles absence. Spurs cruised in Game 3 opener 111-98 at home court, leveraging paint dominance (52.1 PPG edge) and transition scoring, but Wembanyama's questionable tag for tonight's Portland tilt shifts dynamics toward a closer matchup. Blazers boast clean injury slate otherwise, while Spurs' Jordan McLaughlin remains out (ankle); home/road split, recent head-to-head Spurs edge (8-3 past three seasons), and series momentum now hinge on Wemby's clearance.

title: Will MegaETH launch a token by ___?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if MegaETH officially launches a governance token by 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 95.5% implied probability against the Iranian regime falling by May 31, reflecting the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)'s sustained loyalty and effective crackdowns on protests that erupted in late 2025 amid economic crisis and the 2026 war with Israel and the US. Despite Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's assassination in February airstrikes and reports of successor Mojtaba Khamenei's incapacitation as of April 23, IRGC generals have tightened control, sidelining civilian leadership and deploying foreign militias to curb dissent. Recent escalations in arrests and executions underscore regime resilience. Realistic shifts would require mass IRGC defections, a leadership vacuum sparking infighting, or an unforeseen nationwide uprising in the next five weeks.
